channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17517] shift exponent 65 is too large for 64-bit type in lov_request.c
item.description:
One of our clients just gave us this:

[  201.440391] UBSAN: shift-out-of-bounds in /var/lib/dkms/lustre-client-modules/2.15.4-hpc2n0.2/build/lustre/lov/lov_request.c:230:23
[  201.452215] shift exponent 65 is too large for 64-bit type 'long long unsigned int'
[  201.460108] ================================================================================
[  201.468547] ================================================================================
[  201.476985] UBSAN: shift-out-of-bounds in /var/lib/dkms/lustre-client-modules/2.15.4-hpc2n0.2/build/lustre/lov/lov_request.c:231:23
[  201.488810] shift exponent 65 is too large for 64-bit type 'long long unsigned int'
[  201.496944] ================================================================================
[  201.505378] ================================================================================
[  201.513819] UBSAN: shift-out-of-bounds in /var/lib/dkms/lustre-client-modules/2.15.4-hpc2n0.2/build/lustre/lov/lov_request.c:232:23
[  201.525642] shift exponent 65 is too large for 64-bit type 'long long unsigned int'
item.summary: shift exponent 65 is too large for 64-bit type in lov_request.c

status.description: This issue was once resolved, but the resolution was deemed incorrect. From here issues are either marked assigned or resolved.

comments.comment: As per discussion on the LWG call today, moving tickets that do not appear to be essential to fix version 2.17. If the fix lands before code freeze we will update the fix version to reflect that but we want to focus on activities on the critical path. Please speak up if you think that this issue definitely needs to be fixed before we could issue a 2.16 release.
Ok. Feel free to reopen tickets yourself under any future similar situations.

I somewhat disagree with this being marked as fixed.

The broken loop is fixed but the problem is likely not. At least my short analysis of the code around the loop have not been addressed or commented on.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54043/
Subject: LU-17517 lov: fix large shift in lov_update_statfs()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: edbaee798b5e83b0c829cae40419a26f1b0d56c8

Unless I'm mistaken the code should not have been able to go past shift=31 in the first place.

If osfs->os_bsize and lov_sfs->os_bsize are really __u32 and not equal (otherwise it wouldn't end up in this loop in the first place) and strictly power-of-2 values, then the largest diff will be shift=31, (tmp=0x80000001).

The only real way this can happen is if one of osfs->os_bsize of lov_sfs->os_bsize are 0, which I guess should warrant its own check.

(Unless there's a compiler bug causing tmp to be >= 1<<32)

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54043
Subject: LU-17517 lov: fix large shift in lov_update_statfs()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 0f9b91ea25ab1272f0a1915f9ed3a35ecb5702b0

Seems like in `lov_update_statfs()`, the logic

 200                         for (shift = 0; shift <= 64; ++shift) {
 201                                 if (tmp & 1) {
 202                                         if (quit)
 203                                                 break;
 204                                         quit = 1;
 205                                         shift = 0;
  206                                                              }
 207                                 tmp >>= 1;
  208                                              }

is wrong. If we finish the loop without breaking, `shift` will be 65. I think if `shift` is 64, that's also undefined behavior. It'd have to be capped at 63.
